questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Select the antonym of 'TRANSPARENT'.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** 'Transparent' means allowing light to pass through so objects are visible.

**Working.** The opposite is 'opaque' (not allowing light through); the other options are near-synonyms of transparent.

**Answer.** opaque.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: Compute the average of the four bar values shown: $4, 6, 8, 10$.

figure.caption: Bar chart of four values
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='30' y1='120' x2='210' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><g fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'><rect x='45' y='90' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='85' y='75' width='25' height='45'/><rect x='125' y='60' width='25' height='60'/><rect x='165' y='45' width='25' height='75'/></g><text x='55' y='138' font-size='8'>4, 6, 8, 10</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Average $=\dfrac{\sum x}{n}$.

**Working.**
$$\bar{x}=\frac{4+6+8+10}{4}=\frac{28}{4}=7$$

**Answer.** $7$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: What is $35\%$ of $240$?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Percent means parts per hundred.

**Working.**
$$\frac{35}{100}\times 240 = 84$$

**Answer.** $84$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The square root of $196$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Find the number whose square is $196$.

**Working.** $14^2 = 196$.

**Answer.** $14$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: A triangle is divided by a single line from its apex to the midpoint of its base (figure). Count the total number of triangles in the figure.

figure.caption: Triangle with one median
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,120 160,120 100,30' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='120' stroke='#475569'/><text x='35' y='140' font-size='8'>count the triangles</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count small and combined triangles.

**Working.** Two small right triangles, plus the whole large triangle, give $2+1=3$.

**Answer.** $3$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: The total surface area of a cube of side $5$ is _____ .
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Surface area of a cube $=6\,(\text{side})^2$.

**Working.** $6\times 5^2 = 6\times 25 = 150$.

**Answer.** $150$.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If $12$ workers build a wall in $10$ days, how many days will $15$ workers (of equal efficiency) take to build the same wall?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Workers and time are inversely proportional for a fixed task.

**Working.**
$$\text{days}=\frac{12\times 10}{15}=8$$

**Answer.** $8$ days.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: On a pie chart of $800$ students, one sector spans $45^\circ$. Compute the number of students in that sector.

figure.caption: Pie sector of 45 degrees
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 170 175' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='85' cy='80' r='60' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M85 80 L85 20 A60 60 0 0 1 127 38 Z' fill='#16a34a'/><text x='22' y='160' font-size='8'>sector 45 deg, total 800</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Count $=\dfrac{\theta}{360}\times\text{total}$.

**Working.**
$$N=\frac{45}{360}\times800=100$$

**Answer.** $100$ students.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem: If today is Monday, what day of the week will it be after $15$ days?
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Days repeat every $7$; take the remainder.

**Working.** $15\bmod 7 = 1$, so one day after Monday is Tuesday.

**Answer.** Tuesday.

questions.subject: General Aptitude
questions.stem:
Read the passage and answer.

'Critics argue that subsidies for fossil-fuel extraction distort energy markets and delay the transition to renewables. Proponents counter that such subsidies protect jobs and keep energy affordable in the short term.'

The passage primarily presents:
options: two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies
a definitive argument against renewable energy
a neutral historical account of energy policy
evidence proving subsidies have no effect
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Identify the rhetorical structure (argument, narrative, comparison of views).

**Working.** The passage states what 'critics argue' and what 'proponents counter', i.e., two opposing positions, without endorsing either.

**Answer.** two opposing viewpoints on fossil-fuel subsidies.

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: The stable, self-perpetuating community reached at the end of ecological succession is the
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Succession culminates in a climax community in equilibrium with the environment.

**Working.**
Climax community.

**Answer.** Climax community

reduce the need for air pollution control

**Concept.** High temperature + 2 s residence ensures complete burnout and dioxin destruction.

Complete combustion.

**Working.**
$$15000\times0.1^{2}=150.$$

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.693}{0.05}=13.86.$$

**Answer.** $13.86\,\text{d}$

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = C₀e^{−kt}, with C₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$80e^{-0.2\times8}=16.15.$$

**Answer.** $16.15$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: An enzyme follows Michaelis–Menten kinetics. Reading $V_{max}$ (plateau) and $K_m$ (half-saturation) from the curve shown, the reaction velocity at substrate concentration $[S]=15\,\text{mmol/L}$ is _____ µmol·L⁻¹·min⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">V_max = 10</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">V_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_m = 5</text></svg>
figure.caption: Michaelis–Menten curve: V_max (plateau) and K_m (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Michaelis–Menten: $v=\dfrac{V_{max}[S]}{K_m+[S]}$, with $V_{max}$ and $K_m$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{10\times15}{5+15}=7.5.$$

**Answer.** $7.5\,\mu\text{mol L}^{-1}\text{min}^{-1}$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{2}}=102881.$$

**Answer.** $₹102881$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics at substrate $S=150\,\text{mg/L}$. Reading $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate $\mu$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.4</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** μ = μ_max S/(K_s+S), with μ_max and K_s read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.4\times150}{50+150}=0.3.$$

**Answer.** $0.3$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: A stack emits $Q=80\,\text{g/s}$. Using the effective height $H$, wind speed $u$ and dispersion coefficients $\sigma_y,\sigma_z$ marked on the diagram, the ground-level centreline concentration (full reflection) is _____ ×10⁻⁴ g/m³.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 210" width="100%" style="max-width:380px;height:auto"><line x1="20" y1="175" x2="350" y2="175" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><rect x="34" y="75" width="14" height="100" fill="#94a3b8" stroke="#1e293b"/><text x="41" y="191" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">stack</text><path d="M48 75 Q160 64 350 44 M48 75 Q160 86 350 116" fill="rgba(37,99,235,0.10)" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="41" y1="75" x2="41" y2="175" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="54" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">H = 30 m</text><path d="M118 148 l20 0 m-5 -3 l5 3 -5 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"/><text x="142" y="151" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">u = 5 m/s</text><text x="206" y="40" font-size="10" fill="#2563eb">σ_y = 30 m,  σ_z = 20 m</text><text x="318" y="171" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">x →</text></svg>
figure.caption: Elevated point source: H, u, σ_y and σ_z are marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** C = Q/(πuσ_yσ_z)·exp(−H²/2σ_z²); H,u,σ_y,σ_z read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{80}{\pi\times5\times30\times20}e^{-30^2/(2\times20^2)}=0.00275574.$$

**Answer.** $27.557\times10^{-4}$

questions.subject: Water & Wastewater Treatment
questions.stem: In the settling-column test plotted in the figure, a particle settles the marked depth in the marked time. Its settling velocity is _____ mm/s.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 200" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="46" y2="170" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="300" y2="20" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="6" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">depth ↓</text><text x="252" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text><line x1="46" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="270" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><line x1="270" y1="20" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="46" y1="150" x2="270" y2="150" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="150" y="14" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">t = 400 s</text><text x="118" y="165" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">settles 1.5 m</text></svg>
figure.caption: Particle settles the marked depth in the marked time; v_s = depth ÷ time. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** v_s = depth ÷ time (read both from the trajectory).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{1.5\,\text{m}}{400\,\text{s}}\times1000=3.75\,\text{mm/s}.$$

**Answer.** $3.75\,\text{mm/s}$

questions.subject: Environmental Microbiology
questions.stem: A culture follows Monod kinetics. Reading $\mu_{max}$ (plateau) and the half-saturation constant $K_s$ from the $\mu$–$S$ curve shown, the specific growth rate at substrate $S=100\,\text{mg/L}$ is _____ h⁻¹.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 195" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="44" y1="20" x2="44" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="44" y1="165" x2="312" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="240" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">substrate [S] →</text><line x1="44" y1="40" x2="312" y2="40" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="232" y="36" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">μ_max = 0.5</text><path d="M44 165 C90 110 140 70 200 56 C250 46 290 42 312 41" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="44" y1="102" x2="120" y2="102" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><line x1="120" y1="102" x2="120" y2="165" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="0.8" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><text x="14" y="106" font-size="7" fill="#16a34a">μ_max/2</text><text x="98" y="178" font-size="10" fill="#16a34a">K_s = 50</text></svg>
figure.caption: Monod μ–S curve: μ_max (plateau) and K_s (half-saturation) marked on the curve only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Monod: $\mu=\dfrac{\mu_{max}S}{K_s+S}$, with $\mu_{max}$ and $K_s$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{0.5\times100}{50+100}=0.333.$$

**Answer.** $0.333\,\text{h}^{-1}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: Match the correct themes: which statements are TRUE?
options: Basel Convention → transboundary movement of hazardous waste
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Basel = hazardous-waste trade; Ramsar = wetlands; Stockholm = POPs; Montreal = ODS (not GHG caps — that is Kyoto/Paris).

**Working.**
First three are correct.

**Answer.** Basel, Ramsar, Stockholm

questions.subject: Environmental Chemistry
questions.stem: $25\,\text{mL}$ of an acid is titrated with $0.2\,\text{N}$ NaOH. Reading the equivalence-point volume $V_{eq}$ from the titration curve shown, the normality of the acid is _____ N.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 195" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="18" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="305" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="16" y="28" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">pH</text><text x="220" y="182" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">titrant volume (mL) →</text><path d="M40 150 C120 146 160 140 168 92 C176 44 230 36 305 30" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="168" y1="30" x2="168" y2="165" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="3 2"/><circle cx="168" cy="92" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="118" y="70" font-size="8" fill="#dc2626">equivalence</text><text x="158" y="178" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">15</text></svg>
figure.caption: Equivalence-point titrant volume marked on the x-axis only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the equivalence point $N_aV_a=N_bV_b$, with $V_{eq}$ read from the curve.

**Working.**
$$N_a=\dfrac{0.2\times15}{25}=0.12.$$

**Answer.** $0.12\,\text{N}$

questions.subject: Global & Regional Environmental Issues
questions.stem: A facility emits CO₂, CH₄ (GWP 28) and N₂O (GWP 265); the three masses (t) are shown on the bar chart. The total carbon footprint is _____ t CO₂-e.

figure.caption: Masses of CO₂, CH₄ and N₂O shown on the bars only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Σ massᵢ × GWPᵢ, with the three masses read from the bars.

**Working.**
$$100+4\times28+0.5\times265=344.5.$$

**Answer.** $344.5$

questions.solution:
**Concept.** F/M = QS₀/(VX).

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{12000\times200}{2000\times3000}=0.4.$$

questions.subject: Environmental Management & Ethics
questions.stem: At a discount rate of $8\%$, and reading the future benefit $F$ and its time horizon $n$ from the cash-flow diagram, the present worth is $₹$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 330 150" width="100%" style="max-width:350px;height:auto"><line x1="30" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="30" y="118" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">0</text><line x1="290" y1="100" x2="290" y2="48" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.6"/><path d="M290 46 l-5 9 10 0 z" fill="#16a34a"/><text x="290" y="40" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#16a34a">F = ₹1,20,000</text><text x="290" y="118" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">year 6</text><text x="165" y="118" font-size="8" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">time (years) →</text><text x="42" y="60" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">PW = ?</text></svg>
figure.caption: Future benefit F and its time horizon n marked on the cash-flow diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** PW = F/(1+i)^n, with F and n read from the diagram.

**Working.**
$$\dfrac{120000}{(1+0.08)^{6}}=75620.$$

**Answer.** $₹75620$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: An ESP treats $6\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$. Reading the collecting area $A$ and drift velocity $w$ from the figure, its collection efficiency is _____ %.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" width="100%" style="max-width:360px;height:auto"><rect x="40" y="30" width="260" height="110" fill="none" stroke="#1e293b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="80" y1="30" x2="80" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="150" y1="30" x2="150" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="220" y1="30" x2="220" y2="140" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="3"/><line x1="115" y1="35" x2="115" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><line x1="185" y1="35" x2="185" y2="135" stroke="#dc2626" stroke-width="1" stroke-dasharray="2 2"/><path d="M14 70 l22 0 m-6 -3 l6 3 -6 3" stroke="#16a34a" stroke-width="1.2" fill="none"/><text x="6" y="64" font-size="9" fill="#16a34a">gas in</text><text x="60" y="158" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">collecting area A = 200 m²</text><text x="150" y="22" font-size="9" fill="#dc2626">w = 0.1 m/s (drift)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Collecting area A and drift velocity w marked on the diagram only. (read values from the figure)

questions.solution:
**Concept.** η = 1 − e^{−wA/Q}, with A and w read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$(1-e^{-0.1\times200/6})\times100=96.43\%.$$

**Answer.** $96.43\%$

questions.subject: Ecology & Biodiversity
questions.stem: A population grows exponentially at $r=0.08\,\text{yr}^{-1}$. Reading the initial size $N_0$ from the figure, after $14$ yr, $N=$ _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 320 190" width="100%" style="max-width:340px;height:auto"><line x1="40" y1="20" x2="40" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><line x1="40" y1="165" x2="300" y2="165" stroke="#475569" stroke-width="1.3"/><path d="M40 150 C120 145 180 120 230 80 C260 55 285 35 300 25" fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="1.8"/><circle cx="40" cy="150" r="3" fill="#dc2626"/><text x="46" y="146" font-size="10" fill="#dc2626">N₀ = 500</text><text x="14" y="30" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">N</text><text x="278" y="180" font-size="9" fill="#1e293b">time →</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** N = N₀e^{rt}, with N₀ read from the figure.

**Working.**
$$500e^{0.08\times14}=1532.$$

**Answer.** $1532$

questions.subject: Air & Noise Pollution
questions.stem: At a receptor, $4$ identical machines each produce $80\,\text{dB}$. The combined sound pressure level is _____ dB.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $L=L_1+10\log_{10}n$ for $n$ equal incoherent sources.

**Working.**
$$80+10\log_{10}4=86.02.$$

**Answer.** $86.02\,\text{dB}$

questions.stem: In the pH range 8.3–9.5 of most natural waters, alkalinity is predominantly due to

figure.caption: Alkalinity end-points (phenolphthalein, methyl-orange) on a titration curve.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Between pH ~6.3 and 10.3 the carbonate system is dominated by $\text{HCO}_3^-$.

**Working.**
Bicarbonate dominates.

**Answer.** Bicarbonate
